key_strengths: 125% net revenue retention with 733 customers spending $1M+ annually, demonstrating strong expansion economics
$9.77B contracted backlog (RPO) growing 42% YoY provides durable forward revenue visibility
76% gross margins and $1.19B adjusted free cash flow (25.5% margin) reflect a high-quality product economics profile
Net cash position of $1.7B with zero-coupon convertible debt and ample liquidity for a multi-year downturn
Multi-cloud data gravity and workflow-level switching costs create genuine, if narrowing, competitive moat
key_risks: Stock-based compensation at 34% of revenue ($1.6B annually) makes GAAP profitability impossible today and dilutes shareholders structurally
Databricks growing at 57% vs. Snowflake's 29%, with ~40% of Snowflake accounts already running Databricks in parallel
Apache Iceberg open-format standard eroding storage-layer lock-in and commoditizing a key moat pillar
Consumption-based revenue model creates volatility risk in macro slowdowns or enterprise cloud optimization cycles
Active securities fraud class action and zero insider buying over 18 months erode management alignment confidence
At 12.5x EV/Revenue on a GAAP-unprofitable company, valuation leaves no room for execution error
red_flags: Zero insider open-market purchases in 18 months despite CEO receiving $101M in total compensation
SBC at 34% of revenue is 2-3x the level of mature software peers with no credible near-term path to sub-20%
Withdrawal of $10B FY2029 revenue target undermines long-term guidance credibility
Active securities class action alleging management misled investors about product efficiency revenue impacts
GAAP ROIC deeply negative at -16% to -27% despite strong non-GAAP metrics
buy_triggers: Stock price falls to $120-130 with NRR above 120% and RPO growing above 30%, creating genuine 2x+ upside-to-downside asymmetry
Credible path to GAAP profitability within 12-18 months with SBC declining below 25% of revenue and real owner earnings turning positive
AI consumption becomes a clearly breakout revenue driver with management disclosing AI-specific metrics showing 50%+ growth contribution
sell_triggers: Net revenue retention drops below 110%, signaling customers are actively shrinking Snowflake usage
Stock-based compensation remains above 30% of revenue through FY2028, proving dilution is structural rather than transitional
A hyperscaler launches a directly competitive multi-cloud data platform at materially lower pricing, or Databricks captures dominant share of AI workloads
valuation_summary: At $168/share (12.5x EV/Revenue), Snowflake is roughly fairly valued against a base-case intrinsic value of $165-180, but requires near-perfect execution on growth, margin expansion, and SBC reduction simultaneously with no margin of safety; the stock needs to fall to $120-130 before offering a compelling entry point with 2x+ asymmetry.
source_summary: Analysis based on web searches conducted March 6, 2026 covering Snowflake's Q4 FY2026 earnings (reported February 25, 2026), public filings, investor presentations, and financial data aggregators; no user-provided context files were used.
